description_long: This object is a cluster of delicate white flowers with yellow centers, each flower featuring multiple elongated petals radiating symmetrically from the core. The flowers are attached to slender, green stems that branch out in various directions, giving the arrangement a natural, slightly wild appearance. The stems are interspersed with narrow, lanceolate green leaves that add texture and contrast to the predominantly white blossoms. The petals are thin and slightly curved, with some overlapping and subtle variations in orientation, contributing to a lifelike, organic feel. The overall structure is airy and open, with visible gaps between the stems and flowers, allowing light to pass through. This type of floral arrangement is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as decorative plantings along sidewalks and public spaces.
description: A cluster of white-petaled flowers with yellow centers, supported by slender green stems and narrow leaves. The arrangement is airy, natural, and visually delicate.
description_view_0: Several white flowers with yellow centers are visible, with stems and leaves spreading outward and some flowers partially obscured.
description_view_1: A more frontal view showing the central cluster of flowers, with the majority of blossoms facing forward and leaves radiating around them.
description_view_2: The flowers are seen at an angle, revealing the depth of the arrangement and the overlapping stems and leaves.
description_view_3: This view highlights the largest flower in the foreground, with other blossoms and leaves fanning out behind it.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration around the yellow center, with each flower displaying a single whorl of elongated, slightly overlapping petals. The arrangement is symmetrical and typical of daisy-like flowers.
